location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Tuesdays, Thursdays, and a third flex day); up to 4 weeks remote per year

level_signals: prior Staff or equivalent senior PM experience preferred
Comp range $220K-$302.5K base, consistent with Staff PM
Accountable for high single digit % Contribution Profit uplift
Presents tradeoffs and data-driven recommendations to senior leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years product management experience; prior Staff or equivalent senior PM experience preferred.
Demonstrated experience on marketplace products and financial technology (payment processors, routing strategies, merchant economics, or customer/merchant capital programs).
Demonstrated track record of excelling in working with operational teams such as finance, payments, underwriting/IDV, risk/recovery or operations products - both as users and partners.
Deep comfort with experiment design (A/B, holdouts), causal inference, cohort analysis, and metric attribution; ability to translate analysis into product decisions.
Proven ability to lead complex, cross-org initiatives and influence senior stakeholders (Finance, Risk, Legal, CX, Ops, Eng).
Track record of shipping products tied to measurable business outcomes (contribution profit, GMV, LTV, cost-to-serve).
Track record of partnering effectively with Design, Research, Data, and Engineering to bring elegant, performant solutions to market.
Passionate about understanding user needs, behaviors, and decision-making processes to design products that create measurable customer and business value.
Exceptional communicator and collaborator, able to influence across technical and creative disciplines and align diverse stakeholders from peers to executives around a shared vision.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience managing and prioritizing across portfolios of different products.
Experience with machine learning/ artificial intelligence.
Familiarity with B2B marketplace flows, returns/resale flows, or logistics.
prior Staff or equivalent senior PM experience preferred.

scope.surface: FinTech & Ops — payments infrastructure, risk/identity/underwriting, customer support, returns/resale, shared technology and experimentation infrastructure
scope.business_model: B2B wholesale marketplace (retailers + brands), mixed platform and financial services
core_responsibilities: Define a 6–12 month roadmap balancing efficiency experiments across the portfolio with growth investments and platform investments
Lead rigorous A/B, cohort and holdout experiments with pre-specified success criteria
Co-own returns/markdowns, underwriting and IDV policy design, payment routing rules, limit adjustments
Execute multi-provider routing and adoption of lower-cost payment rails
Reduce contact rate without harming customer satisfaction by expanding virtual assistant capabilities
Redesign and implement improved returns policies and partner on liquidation/resale flows
Use a documented rubric to decide when to automate vs. invest in human-driven growth
Empower operational teams to iterate on launches without consistent product development support
Represent product development organization in key operational forums

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($220K–$302.5K base)
Clear dual-mandate scope with explicit outcome accountability (contribution profit uplift)
Specific product surface areas named (not vague)
Backed by top-tier investors; founded by Square alumni
Hybrid policy clearly described (3 days/week + 4 weeks remote flexibility)
red_flags: Hybrid role limited to San Francisco, CA — eliminates remote candidates
Very broad portfolio (payments, risk, IDV, support, returns, resale, shared infra) — may indicate under-resourced product org or high ambiguity
8+ years required with Staff-level expectation is a high bar; comp top end (~$302.5K) is below market for some Staff PM benchmarks in SF
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned anywhere in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff PM', comp range, and 8+ years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred false from JD language focusing on influence rather than people management)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed as a standalone metric section)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from SF-specific salary band with no other cities listed for this role)
ambiguities: 'prior Staff or equivalent senior PM experience preferred' appears in the Required section but uses language ('preferred') that suggests it may be a nice-to-have; extracted as soft/nice-to-have accordingly, while the 8+ years hard requirement is kept as must-have.

The JD lists offices in San Francisco, Kitchener-Waterloo, Toronto, London, and New York as company locations, but the salary band only references San Francisco. It is unclear if the role can be based in other offices; treated as SF-only.
